entities.name: RAINFED AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Agriculture that relies primarily on rainfall for water supply.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID AND DRY SUB-HUMID SAVANNAH AND STEPPE REGIONS
entities.description: Global hotspots characterized by water constraints, malnourishment, and poverty.

entities.name: WATER RELATED CONSTRAINTS
entities.description: Limitations in water availability or management that hinder food production.

entities.name: FOOD PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of growing crops and raising livestock for food.

entities.name: MALNOURISHMENT
entities.description: A deficiency of essential nutrients in the diet.

entities.name: POVERTY
entities.description: The state of lacking sufficient financial resources for basic needs.

entities.name: WATER INVESTMENTS
entities.description: Financial and resource allocations aimed at improving water management in agriculture.

entities.name: YIELD GAPS
entities.description: The difference between the potential yield of a crop and the actual yield achieved.

entities.name: WATER MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The process of planning, developing, and managing water, soils, and crops.

entities.name: WATER HARVESTING SYSTEMS
entities.description: Technologies used to collect and store rainwater for later use.

entities.name: SUPPLEMENTAL IRRIGATION
entities.description: The application of additional water to crops during dry spells.

entities.name: INTEGRATED WATER RESOURCE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A paradigm for the coordinated development and management of water, land, and related resources.

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: Precipitation that serves as the primary water source for rainfed agriculture.

entities.name: GREEN WATER RESOURCES
entities.description: Water stored in the soil that is available for plant uptake.

entities.name: BLUE WATER RESOURCES
entities.description: Liquid water found in rivers, lakes, and aquifers, including local runoff.

entities.name: IRRIGATED AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Agriculture that uses artificial systems to apply water to crops.

entities.name: MESO-CATCHMENT SCALE
entities.description: An intermediate geographical scale for water investment and management.

entities.name: BASIN LEVEL
entities.description: A large-scale geographical area used for water resource management.

entities.name: FARMER'S FIELD
entities.description: The smallest scale of agricultural land management.

entities.name: RESILIENCE
entities.description: The capacity of agricultural systems to withstand and recover from water-related shocks.

entities.name: SMALLSCALE AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS
entities.description: Agricultural production systems operated on a small scale.

entities.name: ECOSYSTEM FUNCTIONS AND SERVICES
entities.description: The benefits provided by ecosystems to humans and the environment.
